items.definition_en: A menu shows the food and drinks you can order in a restaurant.
items.example_en: The waiter gave us a menu to look at.

items.definition_en: A waiter is a person who serves food and drinks in a restaurant.
items.example_en: The waiter brought us our drinks quickly.

items.definition_en: A waitress is a woman who serves food and drinks in a restaurant.
items.example_en: The waitress brought us our drinks quickly.

items.definition_en: A table is where people sit and eat in a restaurant.
items.example_en: We found a table near the window.

items.definition_en: A bill is the piece of paper that shows what you have to pay at a restaurant.
items.example_en: We asked for the bill after we finished eating.

items.definition_en: A dish is a type of food prepared in a particular way.
items.example_en: My favorite dish at this restaurant is the pasta.

items.definition_en: An order is the food and drinks that someone asks for in a restaurant.
items.example_en: Our order arrived after a short wait.

items.definition_en: A reservation is when you book a table at a restaurant before you go.
items.example_en: We made a reservation for six o'clock.

items.definition_en: A customer is a person who buys food or drinks in a restaurant.
items.example_en: Every customer was given a glass of water.

items.definition_en: A meal is the food you eat at one time, like lunch or dinner.
items.example_en: We had a big meal with friends last night.

items.definition_en: A chef is a person who cooks food in a restaurant.
items.example_en: The chef prepared a special dish for us.

items.definition_en: An appetizer is a small dish you eat before the main meal.
items.example_en: We chose soup as our appetizer.

items.definition_en: A main course is the biggest part of a meal at a restaurant.
items.example_en: I ordered chicken for my main course.

items.definition_en: A dessert is a sweet food you eat at the end of a meal.
items.example_en: She had ice cream for dessert.

items.definition_en: A tip is extra money you give to someone for their good service.
items.example_en: He left a tip for the friendly waiter.

items.definition_en: A drink is a liquid that you can have with your food.
items.example_en: Would you like a drink with your meal?

items.definition_en: A fork is a tool you use to eat your food.
items.example_en: She asked for a new fork because hers fell.

items.definition_en: A knife is a tool you use to cut food.
items.example_en: He used a knife to cut his steak.

items.definition_en: A plate is a flat dish you put food on.
items.example_en: She put the salad on a clean plate.

items.definition_en: A glass is a container you use to drink from.
items.example_en: He drank water from a tall glass.

items.definition_en: A spoon is a tool you use to eat soup or other soft foods.
items.example_en: She ate her soup with a spoon.
